creator: frostygoth@hotmail.com
text:
While browsing packages I saw that the comment metadata for the openvas9 package was the same as the openvas8 package. This is probably a typo and although it's minor I believe should be fixed to reduce confusion between the two ports. 

To be clear, I think the Comment metadata for the pkg openvas9-9.0_3 should read "openvas 9 vulnerability scanner (metaport)" instead of "openvas 8 vulnerability scanner (metaport)"

I don't know how to resolve the issue myself, but with some guidance I'd be more than happy to fix a one character typo in the package. See below for the output from 'pkg search -f openvas9-9.0_3'

###########################################################

openvas9-9.0_3
Name           : openvas9
Version        : 9.0_3
Origin         : security/openvas9
Architecture   : FreeBSD:12:*
Prefix         : /usr/local
Repository     : FreeBSD [pkg+http://pkg.FreeBSD.org/FreeBSD:12:amd64/quarterly]
Categories     : security
Licenses       : 
Maintainer     : acm@FreeBSD.org
WWW            : http://www.openvas.org
Comment        : openvas 8 vulnerability scanner (metaport)
Annotations    :
Flat size      : 0.00B
Pkg size       : 1.16KiB
Description    :
This is the metaport to install OpenVAS 9 applications and libraries.

WWW: http://www.openvas.org

#####################################################################

p.s. First bug report.
